profile.title: Profile
profile.subtitle: Manage your API keys and preferred model.

devlog.title: Dev Log
devlog.subtitle: CodeQuest development journey — decisions, bugs and strategy changes.

setup.title: Initial project setup
setup.description: Project initialized with Vite + React + TypeScript. TailwindCSS configured with custom theme (background, surface, amber), Firebase, TanStack Router with file-based routing and TanStack Query.

data_layer.title: Data layer — types, hooks, stores and AI services
data_layer.description: Foundation created: TypeScript types for Tutorial and Step, useAuth hook with Firebase Google Login, Zustand store for the editor, and first AI service (Gemini) for generating structured JSON tutorials.
decisions: Zustand chosen for local UI state — simpler than Context for synchronized state
Gemini as default model because it's free, with Claude and OpenAI support via user-provided keys

routing.title: Routing layer — root, index, tutorial and profile
routing.description: Route structure with TanStack Router: __root.tsx with QueryClientProvider and AuthProvider, dynamic tutorial route (/tutorial/$id), and profile route.

queries.title: Query layer — TanStack Query + Firestore
queries.description: Queries implemented with TanStack Query: AI tutorial generation, user profile fetch from Firestore, and per-tutorial progress. Query options pattern for reuse.

tailwind_config.title: ESLint, fonts and Tailwind theme configuration
tailwind_config.description: JetBrains Mono as monospace font, Inter as sans-serif. Color tokens defined via CSS custom properties in Tailwind v4 @theme: background #0d0f14, surface #13161e, amber #f5a623.
decisions: CSS custom properties in @theme (Tailwind v4) instead of tailwind.config.js — no separate config file needed
JetBrains Mono chosen as it is the logo font and used in code blocks

components.title: Layout, tutorial and editor components
components.description: Component layer created: Header with navigation, TutorialStep with ConceptBlock and ChallengeBlock, MonacoWrapper for code editing and OutputPanel for result display.

ui_refactor.title: UI refactor — full-viewport layout and Run/Verify editor
ui_refactor.description: Redesigned to full-viewport layout: left content panel + right editor panel side-by-side. Separate Run and Verify buttons. Collapsible StepNav.

animations.title: Animations with Motion
animations.description: Animated transitions between tutorial steps using Motion (AnimatePresence). Completion button with visual feedback.

dashboard.title: Dashboard and Firestore tutorial persistence
dashboard.description: Route /dashboard with a grid of tutorials generated by the user. Persistence in Firestore with subcollection /users/{uid}/tutorials. Per-step progress with markStepComplete.
bugs: Permission denied on saveTutorial: match /tutorials/{id} rule was nested in the wrong block (outside /users/{userId})
Permission denied on markStepComplete: match /progress/{id} block accidentally removed from security rules
isValidUserProgress failing: serverTimestamp() cannot be validated as 'is timestamp' in rules — validator removed

left_panel.title: Light left panel with Example and Challenge sections
left_panel.description: Tutorial content panel with light background (amber-50) for the example block and dark background kept on the challenge block — visually differentiating the two content types. 'Example' and 'Challenge' labels added. Divider between blocks.
decisions: ChallengeBlock keeps dark background to differentiate from the example — user suggestion

auth.title: Multi-provider auth: GitHub + email, animated drawer and avatar
auth.description: LoginDrawer with animated slide-in (Motion). Support for Google, GitHub and email/password. Avatar in Header with fallback to name initial. On sign out: editorStore is reset and user is redirected to /.
bugs: GitHubIcon does not exist in lucide-react — replaced with inline SVG
Name mismatch: hook exports signInWithGitHub, component was calling signInWithGithub
Duplicate state in LoginDrawer: had individual name/email/password states AND formData — individual states removed
onChange={() => handleChange} in Form.tsx — correct is onChange={handleChange}
Avatar broke when photoURL exists but image fails — added onError handler and imgError state
auth/operation-not-allowed for email — Email/Password was not enabled in Firebase Console
decisions: authStore (Zustand) created to control drawer open/close from any component

cloudinary.title: Photo upload via Cloudinary and profile editing
cloudinary.description: Profile form with photo upload, name editing and API key configuration (Anthropic, OpenAI) and preferred model. Photo sent to Cloudinary via unsigned upload preset.
decisions: Cloudinary chosen over Firebase Storage — Firebase Storage requires Blaze plan (paid), Cloudinary has a generous free tier

landing_redesign.title: Landing page redesign with component architecture
landing_redesign.description: Landing page refactored from a 183-line file into 5 independent components: HeroBadge, HeroHeadline, TopicSearch, ApiKeysCard and FeatureCards. Larger and more impactful typography.

ai_verify.title: AI solution verification and language auto-detection
ai_verify.description: The 'Verify Solution' button sends code + challenge to the user's configured model (Gemini/Claude/OpenAI). Feedback shown in OutputPanel with status: correct (green), partial (amber) or incorrect (red). Monaco language auto-detected from tutorial topic.
bugs: Monaco did not natively support TypeScript via new Function() — added sucrase to transpile before execution
decisions: Run remains manual — user executes the code and then clicks Verify
verifyService decoupled from the component — receives model, keys and code as pure parameters

i18n_feature.title: Internationalization with 6 languages
i18n_feature.description: Support for PT-BR, EN, ES, DE, EL (Greek) and PL (Polish) via react-i18next + i18next-browser-languagedetector. 122 strings extracted from 22 files. LanguageSwitcher dropdown in Header. Automatic browser language detection.

error_screen.title: Error screen and 404 page
error_screen.description: ErrorScreen inspired by Colorlib-404-3 design: giant 'Oops!' title with text-shadow for 3D effect, entry animations, optional technical message and Back/Retry buttons. Integrated as errorComponent in root route and tutorial route. NotFound with same visual for 404.

polish.title: Cursor pointer, stronger hover effects and 429 error handling
polish.description: cursor:pointer and brightness(1.12) on global hover for all buttons and links. active:scale(0.97) for press feedback. 429 (rate limit) errors caught in AI services and displayed with a friendly message.

tests.title: Vitest setup with unit tests
tests.description: Vitest configured with jsdom and @testing-library/jest-dom. vitest.config.ts separated from vite.config.ts (type incompatibility between Vite 6/rolldown and Vitest). Tests for serviceErrors, NotFound, ErrorScreen and friendlyError.
bugs: defineConfig from vitest/config conflicts with Vite 6 (rolldown) plugins — required as any on plugins
getByText('404') fails because each digit is a separate <span> — solution: textContent matcher
vi.mock('react-i18next') incomplete: i18n/index.ts calls initReactI18next which was missing from the mock
decisions: vitest.config.ts separated from vite.config.ts to avoid Vite 6 type conflicts
Only the react() plugin in vitest.config — tanstackRouter and tailwindcss are build plugins, unnecessary in tests

design_system.title: Design system page
design_system.description: Route /design with visual token documentation: color palette with swatches, typographic scale (sans and mono), font weights, button variants, badges, inputs, cards, spacing and border radius.

herobadge.title: HeroBadge repositioned to the Header
herobadge.description: Badge 'AI-Powered Interactive Learning' moved to sit next to the logo in the Header. Behavior: displays only the icon with a border; hover expands the text with a max-width animation. Previously centered in the landing page, taking up unnecessary space.
